title: The fork in the road, choosing the new path
subtitle: A visualisation for the moment you decide to do money differently from the family before you.
excerpt: A ten-minute visualisation for when you are actively working to break a family money pattern. The image gives you a choice point: the familiar path and the new one, side by side.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, developed for the fear-of-repetition theme of Day 11 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Uses choice-point visualisation from the public-domain coaching tradition.

content.text: A ten-minute visualisation for the moment of choice between the inherited path and your own. The image is a fork: not dramatic, not metaphorical. Two paths, visible at the same time. You choose one.
Sit or lie down. Eyes closed. Three slow breaths.
Picture yourself standing at a fork in a path. To the left is a path that looks familiar. You know this path. You have seen it walked before. It leads to the money story you are afraid of repeating.
To the right is a path you have not walked. It is newer. Less worn. You don't know yet exactly where it goes, but it doesn't lead to the same place as the left-hand path.
Notice the pull to the left: the familiar pull, the pull of what you know, the pull of not having to do anything new. Notice it without following it.
Now turn right. Take one step onto the new path. Then another. Notice the ground underfoot: it is solid. It holds you. The path is walkable.
Walk forward for a few paces. The familiar path falls behind. You can no longer see the fork. There is only the path you are on.
Open your eyes when you're ready. Write one sentence: what you are building on this path that was not built on the other one.

Original to homemade.education. Built for the fear-of-repetition theme in Day 11 of Rebecca's tapping program (see Sources below). The choice-point image draws on public-domain coaching and contemplative work.
